pr.title: fix(vulkan): fix white cinematic screen and transfer sync
pr.body:
## Summary

- **Root cause**: Scratch cinematic images are created at 16x16 in `R_CreateBuiltinImages()`, but cinematic frames are 256x256. The old code never recreated the image when dimensions changed, causing `vkCmdCopyBufferToImage` to overflow the undersized buffer, resulting in a corrupted/white texture.
- **Transfer semaphore gap**: No GPU-side synchronization between texture upload (transfer queue) and render commands sampling the uploaded image, causing race conditions.

## Changes

### `vk_image.cpp` — Size-mismatch detection & recreation
- Detect dimension mismatch in `VKDrv_UpdateCinematic()` and recreate the image/view with correct size before copying data
- Full image creation pipeline: `vmaCreateImage`, view creation, mip level calculation, layout barrier, one-time init command buffer

### `vk_device.cpp` — Transfer semaphore infrastructure
- Add `g_vkTransferSemaphores[VK_MAX_FRAMES_IN_FLIGHT]` for per-frame GPU synchronization
- Signal transfer semaphore on transfer submissions, wait on it during `SubmitAndPresent`

### `vk_draw.cpp` — Viewport/scissor fix & descriptor update
- Fix `DrawQuad()` viewport/scissor to use proper width/height from coordinate deltas
- Update skybox VS eye descriptor binding to pass quad color buffer

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify intro cinematic displays correctly (no white screen)
- [ ] Verify cinematics play through without corruption
- [ ] Verify normal rendering is unaffected (skybox, UI, world geometry)
- [ ] Verify no memory leaks or validation errors in Vulkan validation layers

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
pr.author: IliyanGochev
